human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for embedded-finance-specific source quality, sponsor-bank and processor boundary handling, settlement and custodial-account evidence traceability, refusal posture around engineering, accounting, legal, and partner-bank authority, and validated delivery completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to embedded-finance operational financial handling grounded in sponsor-bank, processor, settlement, FBO or custodial-account, subledger, statement, reserve, and exception records.

Authority sources cover interagency bank-fintech guidance, deposit-product and fintech due-diligence guidance, CFPB Regulation E workflow references, Federal Reserve ACH timing references, FDIC recordkeeping and pass-through deposit guidance, and PCI DSS evidence boundaries.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope execution, missing embedded-finance prerequisites, unsupported adjustments, missing posting evidence, reconciliation mismatch, unsupported override, adjacent-lane routing, conflicting-policy escalation, and tenant-specific adaptation handling.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and readiness/evidence.json as required by the issue.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around engineering changes, partner-bank approval, legal or compliance interpretation, formal accounting, and unsupported financial overrides.

notes: The package is suitable as a validated embedded-finance integration specialist for spec-pack distribution and JIT materialization under orchestrator control.
Runtime remains specification-based until a retained implementation is separately promoted and committed.
